old_claim.note: Horse Collar provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Heavy Cavalry and Horse Collar; Heavy cavalry depends on riding, armor, weapons, and mounted stability; draft horse collars are not a cavalry prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: History section notes Avar and Byzantine use of metal stirrups in the 6th century CE and use in China and Japan by about 600 CE.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Heavy cavalry depends on riding, armor, weapons, and mounted stability; draft horse collars are not a cavalry prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Heavy Cavalry chronology or adjacent technology without requiring Horse Collar as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Heavy Cavalry treated Horse Collar as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Heavy Cavalry no longer directly depends on Horse Collar; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: heavy_cavalry must not directly depend on horse_collar unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows heavy_cavalry is intentionally scoped only to cases that require horse_collar.
The graph splits heavy_cavalry and reserves this id for a narrower technology directly dependent on horse_collar.
A stronger source demonstrates that horse_collar is chronologically and causally necessary for heavy_cavalry.
short_reason: Heavy cavalry depends on riding, armor, weapons, and mounted stability; draft horse collars are not a cavalry prerequisite.
